questions.question: What is the standard probation period for new employees?
questions.reference_answer: The standard probation period for new employees is 90 days.

questions.question: How many paid vacation days do full-time employees receive each year?
questions.reference_answer: Full-time employees receive 15 paid vacation days each year.

questions.question: When must expense reports be submitted?
questions.reference_answer: Expense reports must be submitted within 10 business days of the expense.

questions.question: What is the remote work eligibility rule?
questions.reference_answer: Employees become eligible for remote work after completing the probation period and receiving manager approval.

questions.question: What is the company reimbursement cap for home office equipment?
questions.reference_answer: The company reimburses up to $500 for approved home office equipment.

questions.question: How much notice is required before resigning?
questions.reference_answer: Employees are expected to provide at least two weeks of notice before resigning.

questions.question: What is the attendance policy for recurring late arrivals?
questions.reference_answer: Recurring late arrivals are addressed through progressive coaching and may lead to formal disciplinary action.

questions.question: Which department approves software purchases above the standard budget?
questions.reference_answer: Software purchases above the standard budget require approval from Finance and IT.

questions.question: What is the security rule for handling customer data?
questions.reference_answer: Customer data must be stored only in approved systems and never shared through personal email or unencrypted channels.

questions.question: What happens if an employee misses the mandatory annual compliance training deadline?
questions.reference_answer: Employees who miss the annual compliance training deadline may lose system access until the training is completed.
